description: Free Muni for all youth launches/expands as a universal program for riders age 18 and under on regular Muni service.
stakeholders_affected: Youth riders; schools; SFMTA; youth advocates
action_enabled: Provides a concrete local precedent for broader fare-free arguments

description: Summer 2025 Muni service cuts take effect as part of balancing a near-term budget gap.
stakeholders_affected: Daily riders; operators; SFMTA leadership; supervisors
action_enabled: Raises urgency for new revenue and strengthens protect-service messaging
time_sensitivity: High in debates about service-first objections

description: Controller's Office publishes the Muni Funding Working Group final recommendations.
stakeholders_affected: Mayor's Office; Board of Supervisors; SFMTA; advocates
action_enabled: Gives campaigns an official menu of options and evidence of package-based thinking
time_sensitivity: Medium; especially useful during 2026 revenue debates

description: Governor signs legislation authorizing a $590 million Bay Area transit emergency loan covering Muni, BART, Caltrain, and AC Transit.
action_enabled: Prevents immediate collapse and buys time for long-term revenue strategy

description: SFMTA publishes budget open-house/community-input update highlighting major public concern about service cuts and noting more than $160 million in savings/efficiencies already achieved.
action_enabled: Supports a narrative that the agency has already pursued self-help and needs new revenue

description: SFCTA publicly details the three-pronged strategy: regional measure, local parcel tax, and agency self-help strategies.
stakeholders_affected: SFCTA; SFMTA; Mayor; BOS; property owners; regional partners
action_enabled: Clarifies the realistic funding stack for any future access expansion

description: SFMTA Board budget hearing on FY26-27/FY27-28 budget and proposed fare/fee changes.
stakeholders_affected: SFMTA Board; agency staff; rider advocates; media
action_enabled: Formal venue for record-building, testimony, and policy positioning
time_sensitivity: Very high immediately before board action

description: FY26-27 begins; official structural deficit period starts if no full solution is in place.
stakeholders_affected: All Muni riders and city budget actors
action_enabled: Creates strongest near-term pressure for bridge solutions or service tradeoffs

description: Potential November 2026 ballot window for the regional sales tax measure and local Muni parcel tax.
stakeholders_affected: Voters; Mayor; BOS; SFCTA; MTC; advocacy coalitions
action_enabled: Only major near-term path to large recurring revenue at required scale

description: Projected structural deficit rises to roughly $434 million absent durable long-term solutions.
stakeholders_affected: SFMTA; city budget actors; riders; labor
action_enabled: Strengthens long-run argument that bridge fixes are insufficient
